Corrélations fonctionelles - MB-PLS

Gaspard Martet - unité EPISABE

2026-04-28

library(tidyverse)

library(bestNormalize)
library(kableExtra)
library(fda)
library(funData)
library(reshape2)
library(ggplotify)
library(patchwork)
library(RGCCA)

correspondance_block <- data.frame(block = c( 
rep("AMC", 22), rep("socioeco", 8), rep("soin", 10),
rep("lifestyle", 9), rep("demo", 4), rep("env", 11),
rep("agri", 13)), 
variable = colnames(dat %>% 
                      dplyr::select(-annee, -region, -dplyr::contains("AMR"))))

1 Corrélations fonctionelles

1.1 Evaluation sur données manquantes

Avec la fonction MFPCA du package MFPCA. Dans la matrice Temps x Régions:
- Aucun problème avec un NA isolé dans la matrice
- Sur un time point donné (une ligne), s’il y a plein de NA, pas de problème.
- Si sur une région (une colonne) il y a au moins 2 NA, là ça plante.

Pour l’instant, on reprend l’idée de sélectionner uniquement les périodes où on a assez d’observations pour toutes les variables.

# functional_correlation(var = selvar_AMR_EHPAD_FQ, 
#                        block = "AMC", time_period = 2012:2024)

# var <- selvar_AMR_EHPAD_FQ
# block <- "AMC"
# time_period <- 2012:2024

1.2 AMR EHPAD FQ

Avec la régression PLS il n’est pas possible d’avoir un block X d’une seule variable donc on décide de ne pas traiter les blocks socio-eco et demo

y <- "AMR_EHPAD_FQ_R"
time_period <- 2018:2022
var <- selvar_AMR_EHPAD_FQ
Nb.Base <- 5

automatisation_block <- function(y, blocks, time_period, var, Nb.Base){
sum.var <- data.frame(col_to_suppr = rep(NA, 12))
for (block_i in blocks) {
  
cat(paste0("\n\n### ", block_i, "\n\n"))

res <- functional_correlation(var = var, 
                       block = block_i, time_period = time_period, y = y, 
                       Nb.Base = Nb.Base)

print(DT::datatable(res[[1]]))

ph2 <- pheatmap::pheatmap(res[[3]],
                          color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "grey80", "red"))(100),
                          breaks = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 101),
                          cluster_rows = TRUE,
                          cluster_cols = TRUE,
                          clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",
                          clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",
                          clustering_method = "ward.D2",  
                          number_format = "%.1f",
                          fontsize = 7,
                          number_color = "white",
                          legend = TRUE,
                          display_numbers = res[[6]],
                          legend_breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1),
                          legend_labels = c("-1", "-0.5", "0", "0.5", "1"),
                          angle_col = 45,
                          main = "Premières composantes fonctionnelles", 
                          silent = TRUE)

sum.var <- cbind.data.frame(sum.var, res[[7]])

# ACP
out.pca <- FactoMineR::PCA(res[[7]], graph = FALSE)
g2 <- factoextra::fviz_pca_biplot(out.pca, repel = TRUE, labelsize = 2,
                                  title = "Biplot PCA classique sur les PC1 fonctionelles des variables")+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 7),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 7), 
        axis.title = element_text(size = 7))

Y <- as.matrix(res[[7]][,which(colnames(res[[7]]) %in% y)])
X <- as.matrix(res[[7]][,which(!colnames(res[[7]]) %in% y)])

# régression PLS

# Nombre de composantes sûr (si jamais un block avec une seule variable)

# RGCCA attend une liste de blocs
bl <- list(X = X, Y = Y)


out.pls <- rgcca(
  blocks    = bl,
  method = "pls", 
  ncom = 2
)

comp1_var <- out.pls$AVE$AVE_X[["X"]][1]
comp2_var <- out.pls$AVE$AVE_X[["X"]][2]

# Corrélations variables X avec les composantes
scores_X  <- out.pls$Y[["X"]]   # scores du bloc X
loadings_cor <- cor(X, scores_X)

circos <- data.frame(
  x     = loadings_cor[, 1],
  y     = loadings_cor[, 2],
  names = rownames(loadings_cor),
  Block = "X"
)

ind <- data.frame(
  x    = out.pls$Y[["X"]][, 1],
  y    = out.pls$Y[["X"]][, 2],
  name = rownames(out.pls$Y[["X"]]),
  Y    = out.pls$Y[["Y"]][, 1]
)

#dev.off() # pour fermer le device si nécessaire

circleFun <- function(center = c(0,0), diameter = 2, npoints = 100) {
  r = diameter/2
  tt <- seq(0, 2*pi,length.out = npoints)
  data.frame(x = center[1] + r*cos(tt), y = center[2] + r*sin(tt))
}
circle <- rbind(circleFun(diameter = 2), circleFun(diameter = 1))

g3 <- ggplot(circos, aes(x = x, y = y, col = Block)) +
  geom_path(aes(x, y), data = circle, col = "gray60", size = .2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, col = "gray60", size = .3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, col = "gray60", size = .3) +
  geom_point(size = 1, shape = 3, color = "black", stroke = .1) +
  geom_point(data = ind, aes(x =x, y = y, color = Y), size = 1.5, shape = 17)+
  scale_colour_gradient(low = "blue", high = "red")+
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = names), size = 1.7, segment.size  = 0.1,
                  segment.color = "grey50", max.overlaps = 35,
                  show.legend = TRUE, color = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(data = ind, aes(label = name), size = 1.7, segment.size  = 0.1,
                  segment.color = "grey50", max.overlaps = 35,
                  show.legend = TRUE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Regression PLS - biplot", 
       colour = "PC1 Y", 
       x = paste0("Dim 1 (", round(comp1_var*100,2), "%)"),
       y = paste0("Dim 2 (", round(comp2_var*100,2), "%)")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 5) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 7),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 7),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.margin = margin(t = -8),
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.3, "lines"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.text = element_text(margin = margin(l = 1)),
    panel.grid = element_line(linewidth = .1)
  )

# Convertir le pheatmap en grob
ph2_grob <- patchwork::wrap_elements(ph2$gtable)

# Assembler les trois graphiques
# Option 1 : disposition en L (heatmap à gauche, biplots empilés à droite)
layout <- "
AA
BC
"

combined <- ph2_grob + g2 + g3 +
  plot_layout(design = layout, heights = c(1.8, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Analyse fonctionnelle - AMR EHPAD FQ",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10))
  )

print(combined)
}

sum.var <- sum.var[,-which(duplicated(colnames(sum.var)))]
sum.var <- sum.var %>% dplyr::select(-col_to_suppr)

openxlsx::write.xlsx(sum.var, file = paste0("sum.var_", y, ".xlsx"), rowNames = TRUE)

}

automatisation_block(y = y, blocks = blocks, time_period = time_period, var = var,
                     Nb.Base = Nb.Base)

1.3 AMR_ES_FQ_R

y <- "AMR_ES_FQ_R"
time_period <- 2019:2022
var <- selvar_AMR_ES_FQ
Nb.Base <- 3

automatisation_block(y = y, blocks = blocks, time_period = time_period, var = var,
                     Nb.Base = Nb.Base)
